ci_support_linux_64_.yaml:
c_compiler:
- gcc
c_compiler_version:
- '14'
c_stdlib:
- sysroot
c_stdlib_version:
- '2.17'
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cxx_compiler:
- gxx
cxx_compiler_version:
- '14'
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
pin_run_as_build:
  python:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
python:
- 3.10.* *_cpython
- 3.11.* *_cpython
- 3.12.* *_cpython
- 3.13.* *_cp313
- 3.14.* *_cp314
target_platform:
- linux-64
zip_keys:
- - c_compiler_version
  - cxx_compiler_version

about.description:
`tinyobjloader` is a convenience metapackage that depends on both
`libtinyobjloader` (C++ library) and `tinyobjloader-python` (Python bindings).
about.summary: Metapackage depending on both C++ and Python tinyobjloader outputs

name: tinyobjloader

about.description:
`tinyobjloader` is a convenience metapackage that depends on both
`libtinyobjloader` (C++ library) and `tinyobjloader-python` (Python bindings).
about.summary: Metapackage depending on both C++ and Python tinyobjloader outputs

about.description:
`tinyobjloader` is a convenience metapackage that depends on both
`libtinyobjloader` (C++ library) and `tinyobjloader-python` (Python bindings).
about.summary: Metapackage depending on both C++ and Python tinyobjloader outputs

raw_meta_yaml:
schema_version: 1

context:
  name: tinyobjloader
  version: "2.0.0rc13"

recipe:
  name: tinyobjloader
  version: ${{ version }}

source:
  url: https://github.com/tinyobjloader/tinyobjloader/archive/refs/tags/v${{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 0feb92b838f8ce4aa6eb0ccc32dff30cb64a891e0ec3bde837fca49c78d44334

build:
  number: 1

outputs:
  - package:
      name: libtinyobjloader
    build:
      script:
        - if: unix
          then: build_cxx.sh
        - if: win
          then: build_cxx.bat
    requirements:
      build:
        - ${{ compiler('c') }}
        - ${{ compiler('cxx') }}
        - ${{ stdlib('c') }}
        - cmake
        - ninja
      run_exports:
        # See https://github.com/conda-forge/tinyobjloader-feedstock/issues/6
        - libtinyobjloader ==${{ version }}
    tests:
      - package_contents:
          include:
            - tiny_obj_loader.h
          lib:
            - tinyobjloader
    about:
      summary: C++ shared library for tinyobjloader (Wavefront OBJ loader)
      description: |
        `libtinyobjloader` ships the compiled C++ shared library and C/C++ headers
        for tinyobjloader. This output is intended for native consumers linking the
        tinyobjloader library.

  - package:
      name: tinyobjloader-python
    build:
      script:
        - if: unix
          then: SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION=${{ version }} python -m pip install . --no-deps --no-build-isolation -vv
        - if: win
          then: set "SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION=${{ version }}" && python -m pip install . --no-deps --no-build-isolation -vv
    requirements:
      build:
        - ${{ compiler('cxx') }}
        - ${{ stdlib('c') }}
        - if: build_platform != host_platform
          then:
            - cross-python_${{ host_platform }}
            - python
      host:
        - pip
        - pybind11
        - python
        - setuptools
        - setuptools-scm
        - wheel
      run:
        - python
    tests:
      - python:
          imports:
            - tinyobjloader
    about:
      summary: Python bindings for tinyobjloader
      description: |
        `tinyobjloader-python` provides the Python extension module for tinyobjloader,
        built from upstream pybind11 bindings.

  - package:
      name: tinyobjloader
    build:
      noarch: generic
    requirements:
      run:
        - libtinyobjloader ==${{ version }}
        - tinyobjloader-python ==${{ version }}
    about:
      summary: Metapackage depending on both C++ and Python tinyobjloader outputs
      description: |
        `tinyobjloader` is a convenience metapackage that depends on both
        `libtinyobjloader` (C++ library) and `tinyobjloader-python` (Python bindings).

about:
  license: MIT
  license_file: LICENSE
  summary: Tiny but powerful single file wavefront obj loader written in C++03.
  description: |
    Tiny but powerful single file wavefront obj loader written in C++03.
    No dependency except for C++ STL.
    It can parse over 10M polygons with moderate memory and time.
  homepage: https://github.com/tinyobjloader/tinyobjloader
  repository: https://github.com/tinyobjloader/tinyobjloader

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - traversaro
    - benjaminrwilson

about.description:
`tinyobjloader` is a convenience metapackage that depends on both
`libtinyobjloader` (C++ library) and `tinyobjloader-python` (Python bindings).
about.summary: Metapackage depending on both C++ and Python tinyobjloader outputs
